request.prompt:
You are a precise data extraction algorithm. Analyze the following content regarding a possible strike from "ATAC" (the public transport company of Rome, Lazio (12)). IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS: 1. Decide if this document is actually announcing a new/upcoming strike. - If it is a cancellation/revocation of a strike, set isStrike to false. - If it is just providing information about participation (adesione) of a strike that already happened, set isStrike to false. - If it is providing real-time updates about an ongoing strike (e.g., "the metro is currently closed due to a strike"), set isStrike to false. - If it is providing real-time service updates (e.g., metro is open/closed during the strike), set isStrike to false. - ONLY set isStrike to true if it is an announcement of an upcoming strike. 2. If isStrike is true, extract the strike details: - Dates must be in 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' format. - Guaranteed times must be arrays of 'HH:mm-HH:mm' (omitted if not specified). - Location codes should be: 2-digit ISTAT for regions (e.g., "03" for Lombardia), or omitted for national strikes. Example Output if NOT a strike: { "isStrike": false } Example Output if IS a strike: { "isStrike": true, "strikeData": { "startDate": "2024-12-31 08:30:00", "endDate": "2024-12-31 12:30:00", "locationType": "REGIONAL", "locationCodes": ["12"], "guaranteedTimes": ["06:00-09:00", "18:00-21:00"] } } Input Content (Pre-processing: dom-distillation):

Context Metadata

  • Page Title: Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiuse metro A e C, attiva la B - Roma Servizi per la Mobilità

Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiuse metro A e C, attiva la B

Pubblicato il: 11/11/2022 - Aggiornato il: 25/01/2023

status: 1 È in corso lo sciopero del trasporto pubblico che si concluderà alle 12,30. Ecco la situazione sulla rete romana: Metro A: chiusa Metro B/B1: attiva con possibili riduzioni di corse Metro C: chiusa Termini-Centocelle: chiusa Bus e tram: possibili riduzioni di corse Aggiornamenti in tempo reale su romamobilita.it

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the Transport Strike Update from Rome**

Okay, here's the deal. I need to figure out if this text is announcing a *future* strike or reporting on one that's *already* happening. My input is this news update from Rome about a transport disruption: "Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiuse metro A e C, attiva la B". My first step is to break down the content. I'm focusing on the title, the status, and the details.

The title itself, "Sciopero del trasporto pubblico, chiuse metro A e C, attiva la B", is the key. The phrase "Sciopero del trasporto pubblico" translates to "public transport strike," and "chiuse" means closed, and "attiva" means active. The critical part is the phrase "È in corso lo sciopero..." which translates to "The strike is ongoing..." This is the dead giveaway; it's happening *now*. The details about which metro lines are closed or active further confirm that this is a real-time situation, not a heads-up.

My instructions are clear: if this content is giving me real-time updates about an existing strike, I need to set `isStrike` to false. The clues are all pointing in that direction. Therefore, my final output, in the requested JSON format, will be `{ "isStrike": false }`. This clearly indicates that the input isn't announcing a *future* strike, but instead reporting on the *current* situation.
